value:
v1.12.2 shipped 2026-04-16; v1.12.x integrity chain (#76-#80, #87) DONE.

v1.13.0 in development. Milestone has 13 open issues (actual count, one more than earlier snapshot — #39 interpolation-syntax audit was not listed).

SHIPPED ON MAIN (not released):
- #92 MCP tool description audit — commit 530d284 (2026-04-23). 19 descriptions rewritten to explicitly disambiguate overlapping tool pairs. Core change: codex_get no longer advertises "list all entries" — drove the 48% empty-key pattern in the real-usage dataset. Acceptance signal: empty-codex_get rate should drop post-v1.13.0.
- #93 audit log data quality gaps — commits 0e8f813 (Fix 1 + 3) and 745c5a3 (Fix 2), 2026-04-23. Three fixes: (1) 'remove' op is now distinct from 'write' (classifyOp + TelemetryEntry/AuditEntry types updated); (2) bootstrapRate/writeBackRate labels now say "MCP sessions" not "sessions"; (3) CLI codex_run callsite now passes rawKey so aliasResolved gets captured. Partial on fuller aliasResolved audit — codex_copy's dest/source semantics and other pre-resolve-drop-rawKey callsites remain (see context.aliasResolvedCapture for follow-up scope).

STILL OPEN IN v1.13.0:
- #91 formalize cross-session handoff protocol — context.next_session emerged as most-written key. Biggest conceptual change; design-first before coding.
- #88 askPassword non-interactive, #89 shape-invalid import bypass, #90 hardcoded 'ccli' hint
- #84 entry health, #82 seed-quality lint, #83 topology (Layer 2 tools — calibration data from dataset available)
- #45 attribution, #47 diff, #50 npx, #86 CI bump
- #39 audit stored entries for interpolation syntax landmines

DOGFOODING: docs/dogfooding-real-usage.md committed 2026-04-22 as Part Two. Publishable alongside v1.13.0 tag. #92/#93 shipping validates the dataset-driven planning approach — worth a short addendum post.

NEXT: design pass on #91 (the interesting one). #88/#89/#90/#86 are small cleanups that can ship anytime. Decide whether to close #93 as-is or file follow-up for remaining aliasResolved gaps (codex_copy semantic + other CLI callsites).
